[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0624013313210520342326] Hi Jasento,

Thanks for choosing Transform Credit, we have attached your Proposed Credit Contract with this email for you to keep. This does not mean that your application has been approved, but it gives you a chance to read and understand the terms of the agreement. 

To finalize your application please complete all of the required steps online - https://tcrdt.com/s/3UDDOH6K 

If you have any questions about our application process please visit our website https://transformcredit.com/faq

Amber @ Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Jasento Reyes,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Jasento Reyes
IP: 50.27.123.97

Stated Monthly Income: Retired $1300
Rent: $350.00
Bills: $230.00
Other Expenses: $130.00
Your Credit Commitments: $240.00
Transform Credit Repayment: $223.65
To be safe: $50.00
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $76.35


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

Hi Jasento, you've applied for a $4000 loan, over 24 months, paying back 223.65 per month with an interest rate of 30.00%. Our interest is calculated daily, so you can settle early, make a large, one-off overpayment or regular overpayments, which will reduce your balance and save you money in the long run. You can amend your details or change your loan amount here: https://tcrdt.com/s/X9FM5CEG Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Jasento, we require you to provide a cosigner. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to pay, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you’ve been turned down elsewhere. If you could get a loan elsewhere without a cosigner, we might not be the right choice for you. If you have a suitable cosigner send them this link, they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/TTCIANY6 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi BRANDIE, on 08/22/2024 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a loan of $3000. I have now cancelled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. You can find out more about the benefits of CreditBuilder here: https://transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/. [RE: I did not concern] Hi BRANDIE,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on 08/22/2024, when you applied for this loan of $3000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”.

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you at 08/22/2024 to wintercuddlebug@gmail.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment.

We also sent you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment, to your email, reminding you that your $5 payment was due. This again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.

We’ve been reporting the CreditBuilder loan to Experian, demonstrating any payments you have made. As these on-time payments accumulate, they contribute positively to your overall credit score, paving the way for better credit opportunities in the future.

Based on the above, we believe we acted inline with your instructions. However, we now understand that you may not have intentionally signed-up to CreditBuilder and, so as a gesture of goodwill, we have issued you a refund to put you back in the position you were in, prior to opening your CreditBuilder account.

We will also close your account which will be reported as paid in full and settled on your Credit File.

We understand that confusion can sometimes arise and we appreciate your patience as we work to rectify this situation. Please allow 3 business days for the refund to show in your account.

Many thanks,
Ben, Transform Credit

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Hi Mario, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/H8HFL9LC - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Mario,
Thanks for reaching out. Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you cannot find a cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! We can help you as soon as you find a cosigner. You can reply CANCEL, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/2RC9J0OB
If you have any other questions, just let us know.
Thanks, Sophie@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0600133004072218013007] Hi Clarice,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on 06/19/25, when you applied for this loan of $5000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”.

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you on 06/19/25 to  cmeredith78@icloud.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureau as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

We also sent you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment, to your email, reminding you that your $5 payment was due. This again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.

Based on the above, we believe we acted in line with your instructions. However, we now understand that you may not have intentionally signed-up to CreditBuilder, and, so as a gesture of goodwill, we have issued you a refund to put you back in the position you were in, before opening your CreditBuilder account. 

We will also close your account, meaning we will not attempt any further payments.

We understand that confusion can sometimes arise and we appreciate your patience as we work to rectify this situation. Please allow 8 business days for the refund to show in your account.  

Many thanks,
Divya@TransformCredit

Our interest is calculated daily, so you can settle early, make a large, one-off overpayment or regular overpayments, which will reduce your balance and save you money in the long run. You can amend your details or change your loan amount here: https://tglns.com/s/MF8YQ7X4 Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Craig, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/BIIVCU6I - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. interactions.body: Hi Amber, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/DGTL4RHS - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Tracy
Thank you for letting us know you didn't submit an application. At Together Loans, a loan application is created only when a consumer applies through our Together Loans website. However, rest assured that we do various checks, to eliminate fraudulent applications. We confirm that this loan application is canceled and no loan is paid out. We also added a flag internally so that should the application be attempted again in the future, it will be automatically blocked from progressing further. If you require further assistance, don't hesitate to get in touch. 
Thanks, Together Loans

interactions.label: Application closed — 5 tasks finalised in bulk

interactions.body: Hi Tracy, based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to pay, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you could get a loan elsewhere without a cosigner, we might not be the right choice for you. If you have a suitable cosigner send them this link, they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/00I7KJLC Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Marvin, 
Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. Unfortunately we cannot continue with Paulina as your co-signer.
At this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages, although this is something we are excited to develop in the future so that we can help more people that do not speak English. 
Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into. 
We appreciate your understanding.

You can use this link to invite someone new to co-sign https://tcrdt.com/s/QZEJ26FR
Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Anthony, We received your message about cancelling the loan application. I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. 
 
We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this.   
Thanks, Together Loans

[RE:Welcome to Transform Credit Builder!] Hi Linda,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $5000 loan on 07/16/2024. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/1V82LN7G

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

Hi rose,
Thank you for reaching out. We understand your concerns and I want to reassure you that we are a legitimate lender, incorporated in Delaware and based primarily out of Chicago. We have been lending for over 7 years, across 22 different states, all of which require licensing. You can find this information on our website: https://togetherloans.com/Licenses. We are also registered with Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. We have annual state audits in order to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend. [Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0609083329021905210723] Hi rose,

We pay the funds out to you rather than the borrower for 3 key reasons. These are:

1) To verify your identity and prevent identity fraud from being committed against you, as you have to provide us with an account that is active, valid, in your name and capable of ACH payments that we pay the funds into. 
2) To make sure that you are fully aware of the loan and provide you with an opportunity to return to the funds to us, if you are unsure on whether this financial responsibility is something you want to continue with.
3) By transferring the funds to you to transfer to the borrower, you're confirming your roles and responsibilities on this loan. Additionally, because you agree to make any payments that the borrower does not, we can then use these bank details as a backup payment method if the account goes past due. 

Being a cosigner is a serious financial commitment and it is important that any cosigner fully understands their role and the responsibilities, and obligations that come with becoming a co-signer. We believe that paying the funds out to the co-signer allows them to confirm their understanding and make a fully informed decision, before committing to these responsibilities.  

If you have any other questions, please let me know. 

Thanks, 
Divya@Together Loans

[Notification from Transform Credit] Hi Analissa

Here at Transform Credit, we're a lender but our loans are a little different. Most lenders look at your credit history to decide whether you can borrow from them, based on how you've previously handled your money. We don't do that - our loans are based on the trust of your cosigner. 

We offer loans between $3,000 and $10,000 over 24 - 60 months, in an ever-growing number of states. The loan amounts and rates we offer are state-dependent - you can find our state license details here: https://transformcredit.com/Licenses. Your instalment payments will depend on your chosen loan (you can check the options using our sliders at: https://transformcredit.com). Our maximum APR is 35.99%, with no hidden fees! The interest on your loan is calculated daily, so if you pay your loan back early it'll cost you less in interest. 

If you'd like to check, or change the specific payment amounts of your loan, you can here: https://tcrdt.com/s/PSQBI0TJ

If you'd like to read more about what our existing customers think about our service, you can check out our reviews at: https://ie.trustpilot.com/review/transformcredit.com. You may also find answers to your other questions on our website in our frequently asked questions section: https://transformcredit.com/Help/Contact. Otherwise, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@TransformCredit

Hi Analissa, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/KIY2CXHA - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi David, on 12/04/2025 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan. I have now canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: https://https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/
. The loan is still approved, you just need a cosigner who has a 700+ score and will pay if you can't. James, Together Loans

Hi Anna,  Thanks for reaching back out. I completely understand your concerns regarding your personal information, and I want to assure you that your privacy and security are our top priorities. However, it is a legal requirement for us to securely store your data, as it allows us to properly audit any past interactions or applications you may have made. If we were to delete this information now, it could potentially create issues down the line should you need assistance or raise any concerns in the future. Rest assured, your data is kept safe, used only in accordance with legal and regulatory guidelines, and never shared without your consent.
Thanks, Together Loans
Hi , this is to confirm we've canceled your application to stand as a cosigner and we won't be contacting you further. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi David,
I’m really sorry for the way Anna was made to feel during the call. No one should ever feel uncomfortable or pressured, and I completely understand why you reached out. Thank you for taking the time to let us know.Just to explain, the person doing the verification call has to go through every possible scenario and responsibility, because being a cosigner is a serious commitment. The intention is never to frighten anyone.
To confirm, we’ve cancelled your loan application. If you ever decide to reapply in the future, you can do so at: https://togetherloans.com/
I’m sorry we weren’t able to help on this occasion, and I hope the rest of your day is a little easier.
Thanks,Together Loans

Hi Anna,
Thank you for reaching out and sharing your concerns. I completely understand why you would want clarity about how your personal information is handled.To confirm, the loan application connected to you has been cancelled and no further action will be taken on it.
As a licensed lender, we are required by federal regulations to retain certain application records for a limited period of time for compliance and audit purposes. This requirement applies even when an application is cancelled. During this time, the information is securely stored, access is restricted, and it is not used for marketing or any additional processing.
Together Loans is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS #1905055) and must follow strict regulatory and data security standards. We undergo regular compliance reviews to ensure that customer information is handled appropriately.
Please let us know if you have any further questions.
Kind regards,Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0614180727191327052518] Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out and for sharing Anna’s concerns with us. We understand how important it is for both applicants and co-signers to feel comfortable with how their personal information is handled. To confirm, the loan application connected to you and Anna has been cancelled and no further action will be taken on it.

Regarding the information you mentioned from online research tools, some of what you saw refers to general regulatory requirements that apply to lenders. For example, under federal regulations such as the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B), lenders are required to retain certain application records for a limited period of time—typically up to 25 months for compliance and audit purposes. This requirement applies even if an application is withdrawn or cancelled.
During this time, the information is securely stored with restricted access and maintained solely to meet regulatory obligations. It is not used for marketing or any further processing once the application has been cancelled.

Our privacy policy also explains that information may be shared with certain service providers or credit reporting agencies when necessary to process an application. However, once an application is cancelled, no additional processing or sharing takes place beyond what may already have occurred during the application review.

Together Loans is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS #1905055) and operates under strict federal and state regulatory requirements.

Please let us know if there is anything further we can clarify for either of you.

Kind regards,Together Loans

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

[RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Joseph,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 07/12/2025. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/A5XJ2WXY

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder
